query: As of July 2026, has any U.S. Supreme Court justice announced retirement or resignation? Status of Alito, Thomas retirement speculation after NPR retracted its June 30 2026 story. Any current plans for a justice to retire in summer 2026.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**No U.S. Supreme Court justice has announced retirement or resignation as of July 2026.** The Court remains at nine members.

**The NPR retraction:** On June 30, 2026 (the final day of the term), NPR erroneously published a story claiming Justice Alito was retiring. Correspondent Nina Totenberg misheard "announcements" (plural, unrelated to retirement) as a singular "announcement" about Alito. The story was live for only ~6 minutes (10:51–10:57 a.m. ET) and corrected on air at 11:07 a.m. NPR's retraction explicitly confirmed no retirement had been announced by Alito or the Court.

**Alito and Thomas actual status:** Both are staying. Alito (76) is hiring clerks for the 2026–2027 term and intends to serve into at least 2027; he was briefly hospitalized in April 2026, which fueled the speculation. Thomas (77) also does not plan to step down. Sources note plans "could change at any time," but there are **no current plans for any justice to retire in summer 2026**.

**Context/markets:** Speculation was driven by GOP interest in replacing aging conservatives before the November 2026 midterms while Trump is president. Kalshi markets price Alito resigning before Jan 2029 at ~68% and Thomas at ~49% — but those cover the entire remaining Trump term, not summer 2026.

sources.title: NPR retracts story about Alito retirement: A misheard comment led to the error
sources.snippet: Legal affairs correspondent Nina Totenberg misheard an announcement; story published 10:51am ET, removed 10:57am ET, corrected on air 11:07am ET.

sources.title: Editor's note: NPR retracts Justice Samuel Alito story
sources.snippet: Neither Alito nor the court's public information office has announced his retirement, and we have retracted the story.

sources.title: Supreme Court Justices Alito, Thomas not expected to retire this year: Sources
sources.snippet: Alito, 76, has been hiring clerks for next term and intends to continue serving into at least 2027; Thomas expected to remain.

sources.title: Supreme Court Justices Alito and Thomas not planning to retire this year, sources say
sources.snippet: Sources close to Alito and Thomas confirm neither plans to retire this year, removing a fourth Trump nomination opportunity before midterms.

sources.title: No, Samuel Alito Has Not Retired From The Supreme Court—False Story Goes Viral
sources.snippet: Alito has not announced retirement; speculation continued since he was briefly hospitalized in April 2026.

sources.title: Will Samuel Alito resign during Trump's term? (Kalshi)
sources.snippet: Market probability ~68% that Alito resigns or announces intent before Jan 20, 2029.

sources.title: Will Clarence Thomas resign during Trump's term? (Kalshi)
sources.snippet: Market probability ~49% that Thomas resigns or announces intent before Jan 20, 2029.

sources.title: NPR Retracts Report That Justice Samuel Alito Is Retiring
sources.snippet: Shortly after the final opinion of the term, NPR published then retracted a bombshell claiming Alito's retirement.

sources.title: Breyer Details Retirement Calculus for Leaving Supreme Court
sources.snippet: Retired Justice Breyer discussed retirement timing June 29 as speculation built about a potential Alito retirement before midterms.
